title: Pre-press technicians
description: Prepare detailed drawings of architectural and structural features of buildings or drawings and topographical relief maps used in civil engineering projects, such as highways, bridges, and public works. Use knowledge of building materials, engineering practices, and mathematics to complete drawings. Prepare wiring diagrams, circuit board assembly diagrams, and layout drawings used for the manufacture, installation, or repair of electrical equipment. Prepare detailed working diagrams of machinery and mechanical devices, including dimensions, fastening methods, and other engineering information. (Based on 10 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Review rough sketches, drawings, specifications, and other engineering data to ensure they conform to design concepts. Consult with engineers to discuss or interpret design concepts, or determine requirements of detailed working drawings. Confer with clients to discuss and determine layout design, and explain drawings to production or construction teams, providing adjustments as necessary.

Prepare digital files for printing, using computer software to generate new images and enter digitized data into electronic prepress system computer memory. Prepare and interpret specifications, calculating weights, volumes, or stress factors, and determine procedures and instructions to be followed according to design specifications.

Create freehand drawings and lettering to accompany drawings. Draw rough and detailed scale plans for foundations, buildings, and structures based on preliminary concepts, sketches, engineering calculations, and specification sheets.

Draft working drawings, wiring diagrams, and specifications required for instructions to installation crew. Design electrical systems, such as lighting, and draft detail and assembly drawings of components, circuitry or printed circuit boards, using computer-assisted equipment or standard drafting techniques.

Analyze building codes, by-laws, space and site requirements, and other technical documents to determine their effect on architectural designs. Review completed construction drawings and cost estimates for accuracy and conformity to standards and regulations, comparing logic element configurations with engineering schematics and calculating figures as necessary.

Reproduce drawings on copy machines or trace copies of plans using drafting instruments. Finish and duplicate drawings and documentation packages according to required mediums and specifications for reproduction, using blueprinting, photography, or other duplicating methods.

Produce three-dimensional models using computer-aided design (CAD) software. Coordinate structural, electrical, and mechanical designs and determine a method of presentation to graphically represent building plans.

Supervise or train other technologists, technicians, and drafters, ensuring builder compliance with design specifications and advising on design corrections. Confer with engineering staff and other personnel to resolve problems and assess factors affecting component designs and procedures. Review final layouts and suggest improvements as needed.

Enter data, such as coordinates of images and color specifications, into system to retouch images. Import text and art elements using computer software, and operate desktop publishing software to design, lay out, and produce camera-ready copy.

Produce drawings using computer-assisted drafting systems (CAD) or drafting machines, or by hand using drafting devices. Determine quality, cost, strength, and quantity of required materials, and enter figures on materials lists.

Study illustrations and photographs to plan presentation of materials, products, or services. Draw maps, diagrams, and profiles, using surveys to represent elevations, topographical contours, and structures.
Determine the order of work and method of presentation, such as orthographic or isometric drawing. Modify and revise designs to correct deficiencies or to reduce production problems.
Create designs, concepts, and sample layouts, based on knowledge of layout principles and aesthetic concepts. Develop graphics and layouts for product illustrations, companies' logos, and websites.
Check preliminary and final proofs for errors and make necessary corrections. Review work orders or procedural manuals and confer with vendors or design staff to resolve problems or modify design.
Sketch, trace, and finalize designs, preparing artwork for printing or display. Create layout designs for client approval, ensuring they align with specified requirements and target audience.

Use knowledge of building materials, engineering practices, and mathematics to complete drawings. Develop detailed design drawings and specifications for mechanical equipment and systems, using computer-assisted drafting equipment.

Obtain and assemble data to complete architectural designs, visiting job sites to compile measurements as necessary. Supervise or conduct field surveys, inspections, or technical investigations to obtain data required to revise construction drawings.

Calculate heat loss and gain of buildings to determine required equipment specifications, following standard procedures. Make adjustments to designs and drawings based on feedback and technical evaluations.
Select proper types of plates according to press run lengths, set scanners to specific color densities and adjustments, and scale copy for reductions and enlargements using proportion wheels.
Analyze originals to evaluate color density, gradation highlights, middle tones, and shadows, and examine photographic images for imperfections prior to plate making.
Generate prepress proofs and proofread text and images, perform quality control, and conduct a 'preflight' check of files prior to delivery to the printer.
Examine finished and unexposed photographic plates for flaws, and operate presses to monitor printing quality and ensure it is adequate.
Operate and maintain laser plate-making equipment, and maintain, adjust, clean and repair equipment as necessary.
Enter, position, and alter text size on computers for page layout, and determine standards, budgets, and production goals based on company policies and workloads.
Interpret specifications, blueprints, and job orders for workers, and maintain operations data for management reports of production results.
Recommend or implement improvements in motivation, methods, equipment performance, product quality, or efficiency in a production setting.
Plan and develop new products and production processes, and requisition materials or services as needed.
Observe work and monitor gauges to ensure operators conform to production standards, and set up and adjust machines and equipment accordingly.
Calculate labor and equipment requirements using standard formulas, and confer with management or subordinates to resolve operational issues.
Direct and coordinate activities of production employees, and inspect materials and products for defects or malfunctions.
Plan work schedules, establish production sequences, and coordinate operations across departments to meet production goals.
Read and analyze production-related records to determine requirements and evaluate production estimates and outputs.
Conduct employee training in equipment operations and safety procedures, evaluate performance, and recommend personnel actions.
Keep records of employee attendance, enforce safety regulations, and maintain sanitation standards within the production area.
